title: pelargonium × rotundilobum
intro: Pelargonium × rotundilobum, commonly known as rose-scented pelargonium, is a perennial plant species within the geranium family (Geraniaceae) that is native to South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been utilized in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Pelargonium × rotundilobum include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and its potential to aid in digestion and alleviate stress. Herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, can be made from the plant's leaves, which are typically harvested in the summer months for optimal potency and flavor.
